---
title: "LaTeX for Logicians"
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-before-body: _lfl-nav.html
---

These LaTeX for Logicians pages started life in 2004, before there was such a wealth of general material available online about LaTeX. Internet searches -- and AI assistants -- can now give all the information and help you could need. But perhaps there is still a small role for some pages assembling selected links to specific resources of particular interest to logicians using LaTeX. 

- *[Logic symbols](symbols.qmd)*. Lists of available logic and other symbols.

- *[Proofs](nd.qmd)*. Packages for laying out natural deduction and sequent proofs in Gentzen style, natural deduction proofs in Fitch style,  and similar styles. 

- *[Tree/tableau proofs](trees.qmd)*. Packages for downward-branching trees.

- *[Diagrams](diagrams.qmd)*. General programs for diagram construction. How to produce commutative diagrams for category theory in particular.

- *[Varia](varia.qmd)*. Links to a variety of other resources which could useful for logicians using LaTeX.

Thanks to all the contributors to LaTeX for Logicians over the years! I certainly don't aim to be completist, but suggestions for relevant additions are always welcome.
